title: ReportLogic: Evaluating Logical Quality in Deep Research Reports
abstract: arXiv:2602.18446v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Users increasingly rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) for Deep Research, using them to synthesize diverse sources into structured reports that support understanding and action. In this context, the practical reliability of such reports hinges on logical quality: whether the report's claims and arguments are explicitly supported and can be trusted as a basis for downstream use, rather than merely appearing fluent or informative. However, current evaluation frameworks largely overlook this requirement. To bridge this gap, we introduce ReportLogic, a benchmark that quantifies report-level logical quality through a reader-centric lens of auditability. Specifically, ReportLogic adopts a hierarchical taxonomy that evaluates whether readers can (1) trace an on-topic report structure with a unified analytical arc (Macro-Logic), (2) understand the progression with necessary context (Expositional-Logic), and (3) verify conclusions via

Title: ReportLogic: Evaluating Logical Quality in Deep Research Reports
Abstract: arXiv:2602.18446v2 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Users increasingly rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) for Deep Research, using them to synthesize diverse sources into structured reports that support understanding and action. In this context, the practical reliability of such reports hinges on logical quality: whether the report's claims and arguments are explicitly supported and can be trusted as a basis for downstream use, rather than merely appearing fluent or informative. However, current evaluation frameworks largely overlook this requirement. To bridge this gap, we introduce ReportLogic, a benchmark that quantifies report-level logical quality through a reader-centric lens of auditability. Specifically, ReportLogic adopts a hierarchical taxonomy that evaluates whether readers can (1) trace an on-topic report structure with a unified analytical arc (Macro-Logic), (2) understand the progression with necessary context (Expositional-Logic), and (3) verify conclusions via
translated_title: ReportLogic：评估深度研究报告的逻辑质量
translated_abstract: arXiv:2602.18446v2 公告类型：替换交叉 摘要：用户越来越依赖大型语言模型（LLMs）进行深度研究，利用它们将多样化的来源综合成结构化报告，以支持理解和行动。在这种背景下，这类报告的实际可靠性取决于逻辑质量：报告中的主张和论点是否得到明确支持，是否可以作为下游使用的可靠基础，而不仅仅是看起来流畅或信息丰富。然而，当前的评估框架在很大程度上忽视了这一要求。为弥补这一差距，我们引入了ReportLogic，这是一个通过以读者为中心的可审计性视角来量化报告级逻辑质量的基准。具体来说，ReportLogic采用分层分类法，评估读者是否能够（1）通过统一的分析脉络追踪主题相关的报告结构（宏观逻辑），（2）在必要的背景信息下理解论述进展（阐述逻辑），以及（3）通过可验证的结论来确认发现（微观逻辑）。
tldr: 提出了ReportLogic基准，通过宏观逻辑、阐述逻辑和微观逻辑三层评估框架，从读者可审计性角度量化深度研究报告的逻辑质量，弥补了现有评估体系忽视逻辑可靠性的空白。
en_tdlr: ReportLogic introduces a benchmark that quantifies logical quality in deep research reports through a reader-centric, three-tier taxonomy (Macro-Logic, Expositional-Logic, Micro-Logic) for auditability, addressing the gap in current evaluation frameworks that overlook logical reliability.
